title: The Juxtaposition Thread or Side by Side
display_title: The Juxtaposition Thread or Side by Side
wikitext:
[]: The Juxtaposition Thread, or, Side-by-Side             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 15, 2007 6:04 pm Some side-by-side comparisons between NGE and Rebuild, posted by CtHuLhU in the Trailer thread, inspired me... TO TAKE IT TO THE NEXT LEVEL!!! So I decided to create a spin-off thread specifically for juxtapositions. Image-intense. Episode #01 A. The Birds Direct retake. Now more minimalistic. As noted, the birdsâ€™ movement corresponds extensively to the original. B. Sachiel Under Fire Corresponding scenes, with visible differences. The actual composition is more similar to this cut from #01 (wherein Sachiel exhibits an ATF Jump). There are no apparent modifications to his design. Note also that he seems much more impervious to the fire in Rebuild; in #01, heâ€™s thrown back by the initial hits. C. Sachiel Regenerating Direct retake. As you can see in the trailer itself, the "second face" is actually shown emerging (as it was in the manga), whereas #01 only provides the stationary frame -- definitely a welcome addition. The ichor seems to be obscured by the heat haze. D. EVA-01 Inside Cage A much more intricate restraint set-up this time around. The wall-like structures that envelop the shoulder pylons are either eliminated for Rebuild, or they have retracted. The gizmo in the upper right with the (oldskool) Nerv logo on its seems to be the entry plug insertion mechanism, though itâ€™s hard to see given the angle and all the stuff blocking the view. â€¦Whatâ€™s that long dark grey thing? E. EVA-01 Moving to Launch Pad A dramatic improvement.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 15, 2007 6:06 pm Episode #02 A. Face Plant! Has she fallen to her knees first? It looks kind of weird. B. Tokyo-3 Rising The CG here looks fine to me. Too bad the trailerâ€™s colors are so muddy. C. Psych-Out! This correspondence â€” Shinji giving us bug-eyes before Yui-sama goes apeshit on Sachielâ€™s ass â€” is speculation on my part. But, once I saw the real trailer, it immediately popped into my head. D. Crossroads This cut is really hard to see in the trailer file, but for the time being Iâ€™ll take E-BOYâ€™s word for it. (He said that the trailer includes the â€œscene of the explosive flame that advances along the crossroads in the urban area, which was in episode #02 of old Eva. (It doesn't go up into the sky. Hard to explain.â€) E. Sachiel Self-Destructs Directly revisiting the original cuts. With rainbows. And a lot of blood at the onset of the explosion (no screenshot for that here, tho). F. EVA-01 Victorious â€¦Maybe the wobbling will grow on me.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 15, 2007 6:09 pm Episode #03 A. Enroute to School I'll be damned if these two don't form a pair. Massive reconstruction work, with impressive results. B. Rei by the Window Straightforward retake. The sling is now tucked beneath Rei's collar, rather than draped over it. C. â€œThe Most One-Sided Fist Fight in Tokyo-3â€ The similarities here are very strong. As in, redrawing-the-original-frames-of-animation strong. Compare -- not a single unique frame was left behind. Episode #03 / Rebuild #01 The timing should be the same, so getting them to play side-by-side and in perfect synch might provide some lulz. D. Message from Headquarters Rei gets a close-up for Rebuild. Nice. E. Peripheral Defense System Now with tanks! The trailer suggests that this is being employed against Sachiel, rather than Shamshel, but that is of no concern here. (I hope we still get our fill of Heavy Fighter VTOLs. Wouldnâ€™t be the same without â€˜em.) F. Shamshel Corresponding cuts of her hovering over EVA-01. Sheâ€™s inspired many comments, but Iâ€™ll summarize here. The most attention-getting differences involve the thoracic region. Its internal structure is now exposed for reasons entirely inexplicable, revealing a rib cage and a spine -- but absolutely nothing else. An additional pair of legs (teeny weeny ones) have been added to the caudal end ("towards the rear") of the ensemble, for a total of ten. In #03, Shamshel retracts her legs for combat, but, here, they wriggle around like those of bug trying to get back on its feet. The arms have been altered, apparently resurrecting one of Asari-sanâ€™s previously unused design concepts . The whips now emerge from a different place â€” though, considering they are physical organs amplified by energy, I wonder how they ever fit in thereâ€¦ Is it just me, or does the core look a bitâ€¦ odd? G. Progressive Knife inside Pylon Showcasing a new knife design and a much more intricate and visually engaging setup. Now the knife is jointed where the blade meets the hilt, and it straightens after being engaged. Nifty. H. Progressive Knife Engaged The original cut is drawn rather poorly, so thereâ€™s no contest here. We have the naked hands once again, although they look (A) not terribly damaged, and (B) alarmingly low on the melanin. Sho also seems to be holding her left arm kinda funny. I. Post Bellum Well, we have EVA-01 and tentacles in both, but where did Shamshel go? She couldnâ€™t have exploded, since the surrounding terrain is fine and dandy. Couldnâ€™t have fallen over, or she would have taken Sho with her. Is the Rebuild cut from later on sequentially than its basis â€” Shamshel already removed for analysis, with the tentacles incised at the connection points and that embedded knife conveniently ignored? Another rainbow, and note also the light rain.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 15, 2007 6:12 pm Episode #04 A. Tokyo-3 Shrouded in Mist Okay, so, not so shrouded in #04, but we can pretend. A sign that events from episode #04 may occur. Episode #05 A. Enter Ramiel Vastly different, but immediately recognizable. If you look closely, you can see straight through Ramiel â€” as per the original, but a lot fancier. Episode #06 A. â€œSayonaraâ€ Direct revisitation. B. Ready, Aimâ€¦ The original was drawn quite crappily, so itâ€™s not hard to top. What is that wispy-looking stuff? C. Japan The trailer movie file doesnâ€™t showcase this one too well, but itâ€™s supposed to be cool. In #05, the aerial view is used to depict the blackout; in Rebuild, apparently the inverse. D. â€œSmile?!?â€ In the original, Rei spins around and we see Shinji reflected in her eyes, and then the shocked expression appears on her face. Here, they seem to go straight to shocked. (Maybe theyâ€™re just changing the order of things.) #05 screens chosen to correspond to Webuild. Random #11: Pedestrian Crossing Signal #19: Shinji Being Shinji Does this cut occur anywhere else? It feels like it shouldâ€¦ -------------------- Okay, I'm through.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Jul 15, 2007 6:25 pm I'm thinking the wispy stuff in Episdode 6b is steam. Anyways, good show with the comparisons.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jul 15, 2007 6:28 pm Reichu wrote: The original was drawn quite crappily, so itâ€™s not hard to top. What is that wispy-looking stuff?

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Sun Jul 15, 2007 6:42 pm Well someone's been working on this for a while haven't they! Nicely done Reichu. Reichu wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Jul 15, 2007 6:53 pm It looks like they'll still be using the original NERV logo a bit

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sun Jul 15, 2007 7:20 pm Yah, I personally loved the old Nerv logo more then the new one. They might just change SEELE's and leave Nervs alone. Anyways, Nice work Reichu.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jul 15, 2007 8:19 pm Reichu, its official: I love you and I don't care who knows it! :mrgreen: Excellent work! btw, anybody else notice in the Rebuild pic of Shinji being Shinji, he's wearing some earbuds? It doesn't look like ones for the ipod (not that I have an ipod so I can't really compare), so maybe the SDAT does return? And doesn't that cut occur later in the series, like the Descent Arc (what episode is the original cut from?)?? It was something that was shown several times, I thought? Anyway, that's interesting how they switched the lights going out to turning back ON in Rebuild...I noticed that the first time I watched the trailer, but thought maybe I was just seeing things. ^^; I wonder what's with all the rainbows when the Angels kick the bucket...Oh gawd, does that mean when Kaworu goes SPLOOSH, they'll be a big-arse rainbow to go along with the fangirls views of his (horribly unfounded) homosexuality? :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Sun Jul 15, 2007 8:59 pm Has she fallen to her knees first? It looks kind of weird.

[]:             Quote Post by Vane » Sun Jul 15, 2007 9:00 pm Reichu wrote: Episode #02 A. Face Plant! Has she fallen to her knees first? It looks kind of weird.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jul 15, 2007 9:22 pm Careful, she may make you wear purple sweatshirts and a strap-on â€¦horn. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Jul 15, 2007 10:03 pm Well, I remember reading somewhere Kazuya Tsurumaki did say that the first film more so than the others would look and feel like a mere updating of the TV show. It'll be interesting. Movie 1- very close in tone and look and story to show. Movie 2- starts branching off into new areas with New Character and ANOTHER Eva. Movie 3+4 (A.k.a. Movie 3)- Almost completely new stroy with maybe only one or two scenes lifted over from the series. Question- I was thinking, if Anno goes for a full on serious tone with these movies wouldn't the whole battle with Israfel betray that tone? I hope he doesn't give that Angel the shaft. I'd like Gaghiel, Israfel, and Bardiel to be the Angels in Movie 2. Oh, and Kaworu accidentally gets stepped on by an Eva at a random moment. NOW THAT WOULD SHOCK THE FAN-GIRLS

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Sun Jul 15, 2007 10:21 pm One of the differences between Rei in front of the moon is that, in the Rebuild version, has something in her right hand.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 15, 2007 10:27 pm Thanks for the positive feedback. TriLink12 wrote: Well someone's been working on this for a while haven't they!

[]:             Quote Post by Toonces » Sun Jul 15, 2007 10:27 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Oh, and Kaworu accidentally gets stepped on by an Eva at a random moment. NOW THAT WOULD SHOCK THE FAN-GIRLS

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Sun Jul 15, 2007 11:16 pm Episode 2 cut F Rebuild version is giving me serious EGK vibes. Which is not a good thing.
